summary: Body copy minimum 16px font, line height ≥150%; increase line height for light-on-dark text; use type scale for hierarchy.
Ensure WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large text), avoid color-only cues, and verify all text legibility.
Form labels always visible (no placeholder-only or disappearing); click/tap targets minimum 48px.
Animations require quick transitions, no excessive motion, pause controls, responsive design (phone/tablet/desktop), and max 5s autoplay with static fallback for accessibility.
[GENERAL email] Animated GIFs must display the key message in frame 1 since Outlook shows only the first frame.
All images require descriptive alt text; transcripts mandatory for video/audio per WCAG accessibility standards (wcag.com).
Use 'someone/people with dermatomyositis' in patient DTC materials; 'patients' only for HCP materials; always confirm audience in briefs before writing.
The patient brochure is the master reference for visuals, messaging, and tone across all LISRAYA materials.
Each section is a 600px-wide self-contained table block with 32px top/bottom padding, own background color, and no outside margins; sections stack directly.
Section metadata requires background color, DM status, assigned footnote symbols (†, ‡) to prevent collisions, and CTA presence declared at assembly.
Footnote symbols are superscript, ordered by appearance at assembly; use placeholder tokens (e.g., {{fn1}}) for multiple claim references, not hard-coded symbols.
Locked components (header, footer, ISI) inserted by core team; adjacent sections must avoid decorative elements visually dependent on above/below sections (e.g., continuous waves).
Dark-background sections must not be adjacent; assembler ensures this using declared background colors.
Use HEX colors, px sizing; apply CSS inline only; duplicate bgcolor attributes for Outlook compatibility.
LISRAYA color system: use PRIMARY (Brand Blue, Sunshine, Gold) dominant, then SECONDARY (Sky Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy), then TERTIARY (Coral, Light Blue, Golden Sand, Graphite, White).
Apply palette with primary > secondary > tertiary ratio; blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors used only as accents and surfaces.
Headlines use Brand Blue or White on dark/gradient backgrounds; body text uses Graphite (light) or White (dark) with increased line height on dark, via color and line_height tokens.
In charts, use Brand Blue + Sky Blue for series colors, gray for placebo, Sunshine/Gold only for key figure emphasis, not for non-emphasis series.
Callout surfaces use Light Blue 70% tint for data callouts and Golden Sand 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.
Use Coral sparingly as a tertiary accent only; never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data, and avoid in email unless specified by the brief.
Links: Brand Blue #00529b underline on light backgrounds; white underline on dark backgrounds; colors set by body.link.color token variants, underline by body.link.decoration.
Maintain logo icon's 70% opacity of Pantone 7548 C (#ffc60a) in all uses including logo and pattern/graphic applications.
In 'You Deserve More' campaign, headline letters 'D' and 'M' use ~40% tint of Gold or Brand Blue per campaign_headline.letter.tint.
Tints fixed: Light Blue callout box 70%, Golden Sand callout box 30%, wave top layers 70% opacity via callout.data.fill.opacity, callout.text_heavy.fill.opacity, waves.top_layer.opacity.
Use only full palette values or specified tints: campaign headline letter ~40%, Light Blue callout 70%, Golden Sand callout 30%, wave top layer 70%.
Only use approved gradients (Brand Blue→Dark Navy, Sky Blue→Deep Blue, Sunshine→75% Gold, Golden Sand→White→Light Blue, Golden Sand→75% Gold→Sunshine, Masthead Sunshine→light gold); no new gradients.

Use approved gradients with wave graphic and LISRAYA logo: Brand Blue→Dark Navy (dark/bg masthead), Sky Blue→Deep Blue (blue bg/waves), Sunshine→75% Gold (warm bg), Golden Sand→White→Light Blue (soft light bg), Golden Sand→75% Gold→Sunshine (warm light-to-saturated bg).
In masthead/banner, gradients run horizontally dark/saturated to light left to right (tok_lisraya_gradient_angle_horizontal).
Use Sunshine to light gold (#ffe05c) gradient for Masthead behind logo mark, positioning logo at gradient's lightest end for contrast.
Test text/background contrast with WebAIM; meet WCAG AA minimums: 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.
Use Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite text on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White text on Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy; avoid Gold/Sunshine text on White.
Do not use color alone for meaning; include icons, depth, text, or visual feedback for accessibility.

Gradient Callout CTA: full-width 600px, orange/yellow gradient PNG bg (cover, no-repeat, center) with Sunshine #faa31b fallback, padding 48px 25px 56px 25px; headline top third, Brand Blue #00529b, Nunito Sans 800, ~28px, centered, 0 0 32px 0 padding; button below, 248x64px, Deep Blue #003D74 fill, white Nunito Sans 800 18px label, Gold #FFC20E 24px arrow after two nbsp spaces, centered stacked
Use approved gradient callout PNG background without alteration; skip image only in rare fallbacks, keeping bg-color #faa31b and all other styles unchanged.
CTA buttons must have a mandatory asymmetric border-radius of 49.52px 1.76px 40px 1.76px on every instance, no simplifications or swaps allowed.

Icon Callout Box: light-blue #E6F0F9 box with left-aligned Brand Blue #00529b Nunito Sans 800 ~21px/28px headline, white rounded-square icon badge with stroke on left edge, vertically centered text, no text under badge, on white background.
Badge width W default 80px, inset blue box left padding W/2 (40px), box padding-left W/2+24px, badge left 0 centered vertically with top 50% margin-top -H/2.
Corner radius fixed: box border-radius 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL), badge 20px 4px 20px 4px, 5:1 large TL+BR, no swaps or simplifications.
White icon badge on blue box must have box-shadow 0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18) for subtle lift; always applied to avoid flat appearance.

50/50 overhang uses position:absolute honored by Apple Mail/iOS but ignored by Outlook desktop; to ensure pixel-perfect, pre-composite badge as PNG or background image; otherwise accept Outlook badge aligned top-left; keep Light Blue fill, 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow.
Email body fixed 600px centered; sections edge-to-edge 600px width; mobile breakpoint 480px; multi-column layouts collapse to single column.
Internal section grid uses 12-column model with layouts: 1-col, 2-col (50/50), or image/text split (~40/60, layout.grid.image_text_split).

Section padding sides fixed at 24px; vertical padding 32px top/bottom; vertical rhythm base 8px; gaps: heading-body 16px, paragraph-paragraph 16px, body-callout 24px, image-caption 8px; zero-gap stacking between sections.
Primary CTA: #00529b bg, #ffffff text, Nunito Sans Bold 16px, 14x32px padding, min 48px height, 24px pill radius; Secondary: white bg, 2px #00529b border & text, same size.
One primary CTA allowed per section maximum.
Waves: fluid wave forms in Sunshine/Gold or Sky Blue/Deep Blue, long/short variants; top wave at 70% opacity layered over base, preserving translucency; use only approved colors.
Use waves alone or over photos/gradients to support flow, frame content, or add depth.
Masthead copy must use the masthead gradient device (Sunshine to light gold gradient, logo mark at lightest end, asset ast_lisraya_masthead_gradient_device).
Imagery: Use warm, realistic patient lifestyle photos of simple, everyday activities (e.g., walking dog, beach, BBQ) showing return to normal life.
Images must use brand Accent shape (§2.5) with asymmetric rounded corners, no plain rectangles/circles; waves and gradients may overlay photos.
Every image must include descriptive alt text to meet brand accessibility standards.
Core brand accent rectangle uses diagonal opposite corner radii pairs (large & slight: print 1.25"/0.0625") per surface; large radius top-left+bottom-right, slight on other diagonal.
Consistent diagonal accent-shape corner radii in emails; default large radius top-left & bottom-right across all sections.
Charts and tables use uniform 12px rounded corners on all four corners, overriding the asymmetric accent shape.
Charts/infographics require clean, scannable layout with uniform rounded corners (chart.container.radius), Light Blue panel fill (chart.panel.fill), and Brand Blue header bar (chart.header.fill).
Use Brand Blue and Sky Blue for dominant series, Sunshine and Gold for emphasis on key data, neutral gray for placebo/comparator series colors in charts.
Use rounded, approachable shapes for bars and chart icons, including rounded bar tops, as specified in chart.bars.style.
Chart headline uses Agenda (print)/Nunito Sans Bold+ (email), ALL CAPS, Brand Blue text, thin Gold rule beneath per chart.header settings.
Statistical callouts feature large, bold % figures as heroes with lighter-weight supporting copy beneath (chart.stat_callout.style).
Chart footnotes use chart.footnote.size, noticeably smaller and clearly separated from primary data.
Icons use minimal line illustrations with consistent stroke weight, rounded caps, single-color lines; Default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds; new icons must match style.
Icon colors and backgrounds must use only brand palette colors for adjustments.
Ensure iconography has minimum 32×32px size and adequate contrast for low-vision users in email contexts (icon.min_render_size, GENERAL).
Use generous white space, limit focal points per section with contrast, and break up text with imagery and icons for a clean, uncluttered layout.
Alternate section backgrounds White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, gradient per My Compass; sections declare background in metadata to prevent identical adjacent colors.
Canonical content block in LISRAYA uses bold headline, simple benefit statements, patient quote per patient brochure template for visuals, messaging, tone.
Write LISRAYA in ALL CAPS in all written materials, overriding Feb 2026 guide's title-case rule.
Include ® with LISRAYA® (brepocitinib) on first prominent mention only, external-facing docs; omit on generic name alone and subsequent mentions.
Generic name format: lowercase, in parentheses, e.g., (brepocitinib).
Parent company is Priovant Therapeutics, Inc.; sign-off only in locked footer, never in individual sections.

Typography fonts by surface: print headlines/headers use Agenda; digital/email headlines use bold Nunito Sans Title Case; Office fallback Aptos; body/footer Nunito Sans with Aptos fallback; Agenda live text in digital headlines only.
Email CSS font stack: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; load Nunito Sans via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported for graceful fallback.

Email typography: H1 hero 28px/36px Nunito Sans ExtraBold/Bold; H2 heading 22px/30px Bold; H3 subhead 18px/26px Bold/SemiBold; Body 16px/24px Regular; Secondary 14px/21px Regular sparingly; Footnote 12px/18px separated.
Body copy minimum 16px per brand; never use text below 12px; ISI sizing managed by locked components, excluded from this rule.
Minimum line height 150% for body text; increase to 160% for light text on dark backgrounds, using body.line_height token for dark variant.
Use distinct type scale and hierarchy; never use adjacent text blocks with same size and weight for different hierarchy levels.
Headlines/headings use title case: capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions (e.g., "LISRAYA Targets the Root Cause").
Body copy uses sentence case typography.
Campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' must always be in ALL CAPS as per rule 1.6.
Chart/infographic headlines use ALL CAPS in Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (email fallback).
Avoid ALL CAPS in body copy, buttons, subheads except campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' and chart/infographic headlines.
Use bold for hierarchy/key takeaways only; limit to headings, one key phrase/claim per paragraph, and statistical figures; max ~15% text per section; no full paragraphs or filler.

applies_when.value: brand_name in benefit claim, first instance per section

summary: Bold callout text (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, and brand name in benefit claim's first sentence per section only.
copy_editorial: Avoid using bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on one string, except in chart headlines and campaign headline lockups; max two emphasis types per element.
No underlining except hyperlinks; italics only for study names, notes ('Note:'), and citations; no italics for emphasis in patient copy.
After first mention, use "dermatomyositis (DM)" then "DM"; each standalone section must establish abbreviation unless brief states otherwise.
Avoid "DM" in headlines, headings, subheads; always use full "dermatomyositis" for clarity, accessibility, and web SEO.
In patient materials, use "someone/people with dermatomyositis," not "patients"; say "care team," not "staff." "Patients" allowed only in HCP materials.
Spell out zero to nine; use numerals 10+; always numerals for percentages (%), ages, ratios; e.g., 74%.
Always use Oxford/serial comma in lists of three or more items.
Use empathetic, supportive tone citing clinical data; avoid negative/fear language; maintain trust without oversimplification (e.g., VALOR study results).
'YOU DESERVE MORE' headline uses Agenda (email fallback Nunito Sans ExtraBold), all caps, Gold/Sunshine or Brand Blue, stacked 3 lines left-aligned in hero.
In 'YOU DESERVE MORE' headline, letters 'D' (DESERVE) and 'M' (MORE) use 40% tint of headline's color hue, referencing 'd' and 'm' of dermatomyositis.
copy_editorial: Campaign lockup copy must exactly read "YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.
Use three approved callout styles (main, secondary/data, text-heavy); never place two identical styles competing on the same screen or section.
Main callout: bold Brand Blue text, Gold horizontal rules (2px, 60–100% width) above/below, centered, 16px padding between rule and text.
Secondary/data callout: Light Blue 70% tint box, Bold Brand Blue text, optional single-line icon, 20px padding, corner radius per §2.5 accent-shape rules.
Text-heavy callout: Golden Sand 30% box, Bold Brand Blue headline, Bold Sky Blue subheads, Regular Graphite body, line icons, 24px padding, two-column collapses to single on mobile.
copy_editorial: Use approved campaign headline verbatim: "YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.
Product benefit copy must start with "LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks inflammatory signals that drive dermatomyositis, so you can:" plus four specific approved bullets verbatim.
Mechanism-of-action messaging must use approved wording: primary "Unlike other treatments for DM...", alternate "LISRAYA works by turning off specific inflammatory switches.
Administration messaging must use exact approved wording: "LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.
Doctor-discussion CTA must say exactly: "Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for.
Patient support program named "My Compass Support" offering Personalized Communication via PAL (text, phone, email, in person) and Authorization Support.
LISRAYA voice: confident not boastful, warm but not overly emotional, clear and simple language centered on DM patient impact.
Tone flexes by context, but core voice remains consistent.
Clinical claims must include footnote marker (†) with clearly separated, smaller footnote text as per §1.2.
Patient quotes attributed as '- [Name], LISRAYA patient' in italic/distinct style per patient_story.quote.style, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.
Priovant logo, copyright, ® legal, job code [mm/yy], Indication, ISI only in locked header/footer; never inside content sections.
Brand colors: #00529B Deep Blue dominant, #FAA31B Amber Orange secondary, #FFC60A Golden Yellow tertiary; use in hierarchy primary > secondary > tertiary.
Agenda font is mandatory for all headlines/headers; Nunito Sans exclusive for body copy in print.
Use asymmetric rounded-path SVG (180×59, viewBox 0 0 180 59) with gradient #FFDF55→#FAA21B for internal banner CTAs.
